title: exallage fulva
intro: Exallage fulva, also known as the red exallage, is a flowering plant species native to Southeast Asia, particularly in Indonesia and Malaysia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Exallage fulva has been reported to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever and relieving respiratory issues, as well as exhibiting potential antiviral and antifungal activities. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in the form of extracts, infusions, or decoctions, and can be consumed as tea or applied topically in the form of creams or ointments.
